title: parallax codex-judge verdict (post-green / sole-mode RED arbitration)
description: Structured verdict from the independent cross-model verifier. Two uses: post-green (does the green slice faithfully satisfy the spec?), and — in `mode = sole` — RED arbitration (whose fault is the failing test: code-fault / test-fault / spec-gap?). Passed to `codex exec --output-schema`.

verdict.description: pass = implementation faithfully satisfies the spec, no gaming, edges covered. concerns = at least one finding.

findings.description: Empty when verdict is pass. Each finding is one concrete, independently-checkable problem.

kind.description: spec-gap = code+tests agree but both diverge from intent; anti-cheat = gamed gate; safety = a harm path; missing-edge = real input/garbage not covered; type-quality = public type wider than spec. SOLE-MODE RED ARBITRATION ONLY: code-fault = the failing test correctly encodes the spec and the code is wrong; test-fault = the code correctly implements the spec and the test misencodes it.

detail.description: What is wrong and why it matters, concretely enough to act on.
